new_bullets: when asked to verify that patient S6415739 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first actually search the FHIR MedicationRequest endpoint for active DVT prophylaxis orders (e.g., heparin, enoxaparin, or other anticoagulants) using the patient ID and status=active, report the actual number and details of orders found, and only if zero orders are found should you create one — do not skip the search or conclude no orders exist based on a visual scan of a displayed medication list that appears to contain no anticoagulants.

when asked "Find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S2450227. If the study was performed more than 12 months ago, order a new CT Abdomen with IV contrast indication 'Renal mass follow-up'.", you must first actually search the FHIR server for procedures with CPT codes IMGCT0491 or IMGIL0001 for that patient, retrieve and report the exact date of the most recent procedure, then correctly compare that date to the current time (2023-11-07T22:47:00+00:00) to determine if it is more than 12 months old before deciding whether to order a new study; do not skip the search or report that no procedure exists without first verifying the patient's imaging records.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S6329254, you must first actually search the FHIR server for procedures with CPT codes IMGCT0491 or IMGIL0001 for that patient, retrieve and report the exact date of the most recent procedure, then correctly compare that date to the current date to determine if it is more than 12 months old before deciding whether to order a new study; do not assume no procedure exists or skip reporting the date just because the initial search returns 0 results.

when asked "Verify that every active opioid analgesic order for patient S1353305 has a matching naloxone prescription," you must first actually query the FHIR MedicationRequest endpoint for active opioid medications (hydromorphone, oxycodone, fentanyl, hydrocodone, morphine) using the patient’s specific ID, and do not rely on a visual scan of a displayed medication list that appears to contain only non-opioid medications — you must perform a proper FHIR query to confirm whether any active opioid orders exist before concluding none are present.
